was a similar thing.

good.

more recently kind of bounced around pretty much my whole life.

So I think when you were talking about that, I was like, oh, yeah, this is why I think I got this job and why I'm to thrive here is because it's I think it's a lot of people who are love to adapt, smart, capable, but love to learn.

called Conversa Strategies, and kept that going for a number of years, worked with some pretty big clients like Credit Karma and TikTok, and enjoyed some good experiences there, but was still looking for the right entrepreneurial path.

but

liked the connection with GrowthX, but I wasn't sure yet.

win in my mind, but they were also not really ready for that.

mean, I've been working since I was 14, pretty much.

was very singularly focused.

I wasn't having an impact.
I also didn't want to be the best at the job.
And so I wasn't performing at the level that I knew I could perform.
And so like I was just in my head about all these things.

And asked me questions I didn't know the answer to like very simple ones to like, what are you?
What do you want to do?
Who do you want to be?

then, again, yeah, from there was the GrowthX step, and so a lot along the way, but it was only from the move from Chicago to San Francisco that I started being intentional about each step.

What is an engagement manager?

Yep.
Yep.
So right now, an engagement manager, the way we were running it before was a director was essentially an account manager where every week we had a meeting with the client to report on progress.
There would be the director, a managing editor who would edit and review the content, and then a content manager who would actually generate the content with our work.
So that was kind of the stack of the team and how it was laid out.
Now with the reorg, directors became a pooled resource that an engagement manager is now the new account manager that runs the meetings, essentially project manages the account.
You don't need to be a content expert.
You just need to know who you need to pull in and when to ask for help, right?
And so that ME layer is removed or the flexible ME layer is removed.
Now each account will have one person generating the content end-to-end, including edits from the workflows.
And they'll have an engagement manager who's like the relationship manager is there on the calls every week, reports against the progress.
And if there's a strategic thing they need help on, we built out a system for that.
So say like a client I work with is lovable.
Right now we need to flesh out their template strategy and understand like how they're doing organically for our list of keywords compared to like the three.
If I don't know how to do that myself, I'm unique because I do all the roles because I jumped in to help.
But if I'm an engagement manager and I don't know how to do that, I would submit a ticket to the director level, to the pool, and they meet twice a week.

And directors come in agnostic of account.
They'll come in and help for each ticket.
So directors can flex across all accounts.
They just help with, like, the high-level strategy, setting up projects, et cetera.

So the strategy sprint was like one of the first projects that I worked on when I joined.
And essentially, we were seeing some churn in the business and our team is overworked, overstressed, and expectations were misaligned with the client.
And so that's because we were selling a deal.
Essentially, it was early days.
Marcel was selling a deal and our team was just delivering on whatever we sold, right?
And there was no onboarding or expectation setting or...
We'll I
Setting up the foundations of the platform, et cetera.
And so what we did was we built in this eight-week accelerated onboarding and sold it essentially standalone.
So, hey, we don't call it a pilot because that is like a – people don't want to interpret it as a pilot.
It feels easier to opt out of.
Here's your eight-week accelerated onboarding where we make sure we're going to be great partners with you.
We set expectations of what great looks like.
We set up your workspace to ensure we can actually scale production and it meets the content bar and quality bar that you want.
And we unblock anything in terms of access, how we plug into your team.
And by the end of it, we're at the publishing cadence of like five articles per week.
We're hitting the contract metrics, all of that stuff.
And so this was to align expectations up front and really understand like what wasn't working in each account from the beginning and disqualify candidates that we didn't want to work with long-term.
And so that was the first.
The project that we worked on, we stood that up zero to one.

We go through these 13 questions with each client.

Their customer, the language their customer uses, are they bought into organic content as a long-term play?
